prompt: I want to trace my DSPy pipeline so I can see what prompts and responses are being sent. How do I set up LangWatch for auto-tracing?
expected_output: Sets up LangWatch auto-tracing with @langwatch.trace() and autotrack_dspy()
assertions: Uses @langwatch.trace() decorator on the function that calls DSPy
Sets LANGWATCH_API_KEY as an environment variable
Shows a complete working example with a DSPy module call

prompt: I am running MIPROv2 optimization and it takes 30 minutes. I want to watch the progress live instead of just waiting. How do I do that with LangWatch?
expected_output: Sets up LangWatch optimizer tracking with langwatch.dspy.init()
assertions: Creates the optimizer instance before calling langwatch.dspy.init()
Uses a descriptive experiment name string
Mentions that the dashboard shows live scores and cost as the optimizer runs

prompt: I want to compare BootstrapFewShot vs MIPROv2 for my DSPy pipeline and see which gives better results. Can I track both in LangWatch?
expected_output: Runs multiple optimizer experiments with different names in LangWatch
assertions: Uses different experiment names for each optimizer run
Calls langwatch.dspy.init() separately for each optimizer
Creates fresh program instances for each optimizer run
Mentions comparing results side-by-side in the LangWatch dashboard
